topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the acceptance email, the fluorescent office lights buzzing overhead seeming to amplify the silence in his small apartment. He reread the confirmation, his thumb tracing the bolded words: "Congratulations, you have been selected for the Senior Analyst position." A cold knot tightened in his stomach, spreading outwards until his chest felt constricted. He remembered Sarah, practically vibrating with excitement when she’d shared the news a week prior. Now, all he could see was her beaming face, and the sharp, unwelcome realization that he had just been edged out. He pushed himself up from the worn armchair, the movement stiff and clumsy. He needed air, but the air felt thick, suffocating. He walked to the window, watching the city blur beneath him, the mundane reality of his situation suddenly feeling monumental and deeply embarrassing.

[story 2]

"Seriously? Of course, she got it," Mark muttered under his breath, the sound barely escaping his lips as he scrolled through his inbox. His jaw felt tight, his hands clenched into fists so hard his knuckles were white. He kept glancing over at Chloe, who was laughing easily with a colleague across the cubicle farm, her posture radiating effortless success. Every casual interaction felt like a spotlight shining directly on his failure. He tried to focus on his spreadsheet, but the numbers swam before his eyes. He took a deep, shaky breath, trying to smooth the sudden tension in his shoulders, but the effort only made him feel more exposed. He felt a distinct urge to simply disappear, to vanish into the beige carpet.

[story 3]

"I just... I can't believe it," Maya whispered, her voice sounding unnaturally high. She picked up her phone, staring blankly at the screen where the official notification glowed. Her cheeks burned, a flush rising from her neck all the way up to her hairline. She avoided looking at anyone, focusing instead on the intricate pattern of the wallpaper behind her desk. Every time a colleague approached, Maya instinctively hunched her shoulders, trying to shrink into the chair. The air in the room seemed too thin, and she felt a strange, hot prickling sensation across her skin. She mumbled an apology to the empty space beside her, wishing the floor would just open up and swallow her whole.
